steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: A margin of error applied to a sample mean produces a confidence interval. With mean $20.5$ and margin of error $1$: lower bound $= 20.5 - 1 = 19.5$, upper bound $= 20.5 + 1 = 21.5$. The interval is $[19.5, 21.5]$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: A confidence interval conveys that the true population mean is plausible within the computed range, not that it is guaranteed to lie there. Values outside the interval are not impossible — they are simply less supported by this sample.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: The interval describes the mean of the population, not the range of every individual data value in the population.
